reviews.review_title: The reason i own a quest 3 is this game
reviews.review_description:
Its not just a game. Its a whole experience that is drenched in so much careful attention to details. Day one when I popped into metacity for the first time I looked around and knew it was special. It was going to be special. And one day far in the future I would pop back into this game for nostalgia and it would be thick. I was at the beginning of an experience that no other experience come close to for me. I have dreamed of this world. Years before the tech was even available. You can go deep dive the police job and keep them streets safe, or you can simply contemplate life on the balcony of your purchased home smoking your analog vape with drink in hand. You become a sports legend. Dude there is an arcade. And video game systems inside your many purchaseable properties. Playing a game within a game and getting so immersed that when a synth walks by your window, you forgot you aren't really sitting in that room gaming. Cheetah print is an option for your gun. How bout that detail. And umbrellas are bullet proof. You get a sick flying car right off the bat. You can upgrade it. Its current final form is a beast of a car. It'll have duel guns on the sides for the amazing car chases you will find must be done to protect this city. This city is your world. Its beautifully made. It is a whole vibe. And cyberpunk is my kinda genre.  I am forever grateful to norainu and Alioth third for creating this world for our world. A wonderful escape. A game with player choice. A story that I hang on for the next chapter of. I stand in awe of what has begun here. They never stop working on it. Every update has me more excited than a kid on Christmas morning that got everything they wanted. The A.I. art to me is perfect and the fact that its hated makes it work that much better in the game. In a dystopia the corpos are gonna cheap out and exploit A.I. out of pure greed. They will not be trying to be the good guy. They just want them credits. You'll find out what velocity ball is and become the best player Metacity has ever seen. Go get Ramen at the Ramen stand. Grab a cheeseburger and go play some arcade machines. Go train in a dojo with swords. Fly down to the streets and play basketball. You get to play sides. You wanna be bad? Be bad. You wanna be the best by the book cop you can be, free choice is yours.  There is a train that runs through the whole city and they are hard at work on an update that allows you to ride the train. There is in game radio stations and you will get sour cake stuck in your head. This is a perfect game in what it wants to do. I don't know the entire vision, but it only gets league's better with each update. One is coming soon. I think about it daily. Im pretty sure I came to Earth to play this game. Its amazing. Its getting better all the time. Its cheap! And there is no better bang for that buck. I wish the developers much success. The discord community has been great. I love it there. Great game. The greatest even. And its their first game they ever made. YOU ARE CONSTANTLY KNOCKING IT OUT OF THE PARK, GUYS. Total fan. I havent even covered all you can do here. Warehouse shoot outs, bobble heads for your flying car dash, just so much fun little stuff. It all adds up to the best most amazing VR experience ive ever had. Eternally grateful for the experience. I've had plenty of deep thoughts just chilling in the zen garden at the dojo. Its chill and only as intense as you want it to be. Its real special what they got going on here.No regrets, support these two guys. They are worth it and i believe in what they are creating. Thanks again to the devi. You guys have made the world better by creating a world within that world that is safe to escape to. Its gonna go your way in Metacity. I don't know how anyone could come out of the headset and not be completely blown away by this whole deal. The price, the experiences you get to have. Top shelf all time favorite game. Thank you Norain Studios. Thank you

-DanielVR3000

developer_response.body:
Hey thanks so much! It really means a lot to us to read stuff like this, and to find people who are as passionate about the game as we are!

When I first set out to create this game, it was really to satisfy my longing for a game of this type, and a VR world with this atmosphere that I could experience, I had to learn a lot to make it happen, and really my only goal was to create a world that I could enjoy myself in VR. The longer time goes on, the more it blows my mind that other people out there love to play and inhabit the world that Alioth and I bought to life!

Anyway, we continue to have a blast working on updates for this game. It's a great pleasure making each new idea a reality and having the community involved so much with shaping and experiencing each new feature. We can't wait to get the next updates out there!

reviews.review_description:
I love this game's concept and style. It has kind of like an openworld Cyberpunk/Blade Runner look to it.  I also enjoy how I can free roam and explore the openworld. It's impressive how the developers managed to pull this off. 
The recent update makes it look even better and adds tons of visuals. Highly recommended especially for the price. Cheers to the devs and Meta Quest 2 for making this happen.

UPDATE: Just had to say, this game keeps getting better and better with all the updates! Phenomenal work!

developer_response.body: Thanks again for the (updated) review! It's great to hear that existing players are still jumping back in to Metacity to see what's new 😁It spurs us on to carry on creating new things for more updates, and we have plenty more plans!

reviews.review_description:
I wanted to thank you for creating MetaCity Patrol.

What makes your game special is not just the missions, weapons, vehicles, or upgrades. It is the feeling of actually living in MetaCity.

After several promotions, I find myself doing things I never expected in a VR game. I finish my shift, park my patrol vehicle at HQ, go back to my room, take a shower, and go to sleep. I save credits for a better home. I talk to informants. I investigate cases. I explore buildings floor by floor. I feel like I am part of the city.

Many games create a world. MetaCity Patrol creates a place where players want to spend time.

One idea I would love to see in the future is a space-based game built with the same philosophy. Not just a space combat game, but a living world inspired by asteroid colonies, stations, moons, and ships. A place where players can work, travel, own a ship, upgrade equipment, rent homes, explore stations, and feel like they belong there.

The reason I mention this is because your team has already achieved something rare: you made me care about the world itself.

Thank you for continuing to expand MetaCity and for listening to the community.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review! It really means a lot to us. Creating an atmospheric world that can be lived in was the major reason for me to set out creating this game, as it's something I always wanted to experience in a VR.

Metacity keeps expanding as we keep thinking up new ideas, I wonder if one day it could expand in the space! I doubt it, but I can dream! Regardless, we will keep working on expanding this world to create the best experience we can 😊

reviews.review_title: Now THIS is the VR I want to escape to!
reviews.review_description:
This is the type of VR that one imagines when they think about strapping on a head set for the first time.  A cyberpunk style open world that also has a a fantastic cop game with quests to boot.  Graphics are fantastic, the immersive experience is simply amazing, and it is a great shout out to a time where we  thought the  future was going  to be a whole different place.  

Seriously, thanks to the developers for this awesome experience.  I highly recommend trying this out!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review! I always appreciate players taking the time to write kind words about the game!

This was the kind of experience I always wanted when I first discovered VR, but I couldn't find anything that quite scratched that itch, and so I learned to develop it for myself 😂

It's been hard work getting the game to this point, but it always feels worth it when I hear from players who are happy with the experience!

reviews.review_title: You Guys Are REALLY Rocking On This Game
reviews.review_description: You guys really have created something special with this game. i can do SO MUCH. I get lost in the city. I love walking and window shopping it's one of my favorites. This game has become a lifestyle I basically live in MetaCity nowadays:) the recent update with the music, amazing. All of your updates have been great the graphic quality keeps getting better. I am very impressed and I cannot wait for more. Thank you so much for this beautiful gem!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review!

My dream for this game when I started out was to create a world big enough to have a real atmosphere that is worth spending time in. The feedback from the community really inspires us to keep expanding the world and creating new things to do in it, so thanks again for your kind words!

reviews.review_description:
This is an absolutely wonderful VR game. I very rarely review games, but this one really turned out pack so much punch for it's small footprint in both your wallet and storage, I just had to chime in.

Yes, the game is smaller, so the map is not humongous, but the game is oozing in atmosphere and variety. You can jump in and play it for a short burst and take random missions, or lose yourself in the game.

While it's still in early access, you can tell this game was made with passion and the developers will have great plans for the game. 

This game suits VR perfectly. Sometimes huge ambitious games can be exhausting in VR. This game is really something you can hop in for a quick play, or play for as long as you want, and it has so many cool elements and obvious clear direction about what they want the game to be.

Absolutely well worth the purchase! Hope it sees continued (and deserved) success!

UPDATE: Still great! These updates to the game just keep adding so many fun things to do. Maybe I'll take a break from missions and play some video games on my in-game home TV. The new subway system is awesome. It's actually really cool being able to look at the map in the station and decide which train to take.

You guys are doing a great job with making each vehicle have its own identity with the handling, and also remain fun. So good!

developer_response.body:
Thanks again for taking the time to write and update your review!

The trains were a fun feature we knew we just had to add! We hope to integrate them more in to the main gameplay in the upcoming updates.

I think the secret to a great open(ish)-world is being able to take a break from the main game and explore and enjoy other random things in the world, so I'm glad you're taking time out to play a few video games and ride the trains, it's exactly as I hoped the game to be played 😊

We'll keep working on building the world, and expanding the gameplay. I hope you'll love what we have coming next!

reviews.review_title: Awesome game! Worth a try for sure.
reviews.review_description: If you like cyberpunk style stuff this is definitely the game for you I think the developers should add some more explorable areas but this is a very wide open map with all sorts of stuff to check out pretty much free to move and fly and walk around at your will the game needs ragdoll physics and better AI characters but it's got potential to be something pretty awesome if this had a large multiplayer capacity oh my word your game would dominate the market add some Better AI to NPCS and some ragdoll physics to them and boom you got GTA cyberpunk absolutely amazing experience to do alone as well.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're big fans of the cyberpunk genre and want to cram in as much of that atmosphere as we can 😊We're always working on new updates and more explorable areas is something that I am working on very soon. As well as adding new features and story, we try and grow the world with each update. Development never sleeps in Metacity!

reviews.review_description:
i first tried this game out on app lab years a few years ago and i really enjoyed the atmosphere and flying car mechanics. even then i knew this game had a lot of potential. 

a few years later and im so grateful to see how much the game has improved and grown since i first picked it up. through every update and tweak the game just kept getting better. so hats off to the team who made this game and i can’t wait to see whats next.

developer_response.body: Hey thanks a lot. I love hearing from the OG App Lab players who are still exploring Metacity! We love thinking up and creating future plans, work never stops on the next update!

reviews.review_title: A Replicant of a Fantastic World
reviews.review_description:
When you finally install the door windows and the transparent floor glass into your Hover Car, you'll finally be able to experience those iconic movie scenes from Blade Runner. 
Dr. Tyrell's famous quote about his product, "More Human than Human" applies here, because this game is more Blade Runner than any other attempt I've seen to bring that film into gaming, and VR. 
If you've played Cloudpunk on Steam, then you're in for a treat because Metacity Patrol is better, with atmospheric visuals and more stuff to do. 
The imersion is perfect, from the raindrops hitting your windscreen to the wonderful nostalgic background music.  

But should you tire of accepting missions from Police HQ, you can always chase a career as a world-class Rollerball player at the civic dome or walk around the shopping district and look for broken surveillance cameras. Practice your shooting skills at the gun range, get on the leader boards in upper city racing, or chill at your apartment playing on your 80's game console.
I've spent hours in this game soaking in the evolving world that these hard-working Devs are so passionate about. I obsessively check for recent updates, and I can't wait to show this to my daughter when she visits this summer from the Midwest. She and I are huge Blade Runner fans, and I feel guilty keeping this world all to myself. 
I'd encourage you to become part of it as well.

Watch it grow. 

See it evole into something more than code and graphics.

Experience it breathe, and begin to wonder if it's real or a dream.

Update: my daughter is visiting from Chicago, and she finally got to check out the game.  Her review: "I just want to keep playing!"

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks again for the review and the update! I'm so glad your daughter loved the game too. Were already back working on the next update, it's reviews like this that keep us going! 🫡

reviews.review_title: Impressive vibes, and so much content
reviews.review_description: I very rarely leave reviews, but this is really a great game. Vibes are awesome, graphically it's also nice (it has a retro fps look, which is a good compromise), and there is tons of content! Stuff like sports arena, or a night club, or a metro system with buying and validating tickets, arcade machines, and so much more, absolutely phenomenal

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for taking the time to leave such a nice review!

I really means a lot to us when we hear from players who appreciate all the little details we add! We keep building and updating the game out of our love for this genre, but the positive feedback from the community is the oxygen that keeps us going! So thanks again 🙂

reviews.review_description:
Okay, I know this game is fresh in development, and I know that this is a 1 or 2-person development crew, and I love every moment I spend in the world.

You guys/gals probably have a roadmap too.

And given the obvious use of AI, I have an idea that could work if done right, an interactive AI system.
Use of Characterai or Perchanceai character chats with AI voice-overs would work wonders. (Don't get me wrong, Dispatch and Kira's voices are fantastic and distinctive, plus you'd probably need to get permission or a licensing thing from Characterai which could prove costly) But this could open up a whole new avenue for further development, especially with some mission content like domestic disturbances, where you have to go meet up with another officer and figure out what happened. (Maybe a voice command system to add a new level of interaction! Though that may be more complicated) Or a foot chase through some procedurally generated back streets. Maybe add a feature where if you're lost and outside in a semi-clear area, you can call your aircar to you (I am mostly hoping for the feature to non-lethally subdue suspects) maybe later in development add a booking and processing task/minigame?

These are more than 1 idea now that I think about it...oh well, my appreciation for the game is phenomenal though, and I look forward to future updates.

Update (June 1st, 2026): I've spent well a considerable amount of time playing this game, and I enjoy the atmosphere that's been made...it's to the point where I take a seat on a bench and just enjoy the ambiance.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the updated review! So glad that you're still having fun in Metacity 😊

We're still working on adding plenty more stuff (you've probably already noticed that you can now call your car from any area with a subway station), and I'm working soon on procedurally generated street areas amongst other things. I hope you'll love what we have coming next!

reviews.review_title: "Dead or alive you are coming with me".
reviews.review_description:
You really feel like a cop on the beat taking back what's left of humanity. The game trusts that you will find your path and complete missions that matter. The city below feels like the not too distant future which adds a layer of realism to the story. A taste of whats coming (or already here). Your not tangled in a story plot that pulls you away. You stay in the moment.


Ide like to see hover police bikes and maybe some flying fire trucks putting out buildings on fire. Maybe ambulance back up missions. Im sure this talented team can do it!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review!

That part of Robocop where he is first unleased into the city to start fighting crime is one of those moments (amongst many 80's/90's films) that I wanted to recreate, basically patrolling the streets as a dope future cop 🙂

Bikes have been requested by a few players, fire trucks and ambulances are cool suggestions also! We are always looking for new ideas by listening to player feedback, so thanks for sharing!

reviews.review_title: Good game at a very reasonable price …
reviews.review_description: I have owned this game for a long time now and as I remember the developers said it would have future updates.. they certainly kept there promises.. they also kept the price of the game down so they also need complementing for that  .. very well done 👏

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

Yes, I'm so thankful to all the players who stuck with us from the early days! The game started off comparatively small, but over the years we've built it up bit by bit, and we continue to try and push closer to the game of our dreams. The encouragement from the community certainly motivates us to keep going🙂

reviews.review_description: This game is…worth it!! I havent even done any missions haha. Ive spent the last 4-5 hours flying around the city doing tricks! I turned down the volume. Popped in my hesdphones, turned on youtube from my phone and just immersed myself into the sky! Im sure the story will be just as if not more satisfying than the fun Im having just flying around! Amazing! If youre wondering if its worth it? Ive been looking for a city free roam game..this scratches that itch! The ONLY thing I wish is that there was a day time as well and the fog was gone so I could see the cars, people etc down below and that I could land my car anywhere so I can really park, get out, sit and enjoy the view!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! The fog and the darkness were effects I used to keep the performance steady on the Quest (as well as being in keeping with the game atmosphere), so it would be trick to change that part too much. I'm planning to make a lot more of the city explorable though, and we're working as hard as ever on the next bunch of updates to grow the world even more!

reviews.review_description:
Only two questions?
1. Are the subways in all underground locations?
2. I am only able to see two available cars for purchase, are they all patrol cars and how do I access all four? 
Thanks again this game is crazy constantly evolving which is awesome! Thanks

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

To answer your questions:

1) At the moment there are subway stations in Commercial, Port, Theatre, Tech Zone, Residential and Market areas. We will be adding more lines to the subway later, serving more areas (including some new ones!)

2) There are two cars purchasable from Auto Store in Commercial, one car from garage at Police HQ and one more car available at the new garage between Tech Zone and Archives (look out for the sign on the skyscraper).

Next update is already in the works, the evolution will continue, so stay tuned! 🙂

reviews.review_description: One of the best places to go and take a break from the real world. This game will be somerhing very big If devs keep evolving metacity and content what u can do in there. Thumbs up and easy 5 star 👍👍⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! Yes, we're still dedicated to expanding the world even more. We have lots of ideas for the future, we don't ever want to stop! 😊

reviews.review_description:
This has got to be the game with the best environment that I have ever played in VR. Just flying around the city feels awesome, and even the individual locations can be great. I have only played a few missions, but it has been great so far.

The only thing I am unsure of is the interrogations. You meant to put the evidence on the table and get a reaction, but I never seemed to? Unless I am not putting it where it should be?

This is quite an old game if I remember correctly, but honestly, it feels like brand new. If you have been sitting on the fence on this one, I would highly recommend it.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review!

Hopefully Metacity will reveal even more to you as you play a little longer :)

For the interrogations, you can pile a few items of evidence on the table to give a stronger reaction. For synthetics, the rate of blinking will increase, and eventually the eyes will flash if there is enough strong evidence on the table. If the suspect is human, the blinking rate will not change at all when placing any amount of evidence on the table.

I hope that helps!

reviews.review_description: How I figure out where everything new is in the city? I always check for updates and when there is one I have a hard time finding the new stuff I. The game

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

The new garage (where you can repaint cars) is between Tech Zone and Archives (look out for the sign on the skyscraper). You can also buy one of the new cars from this location. You can buy a couple more cars from Auto Store in Commercial, and another from the garage at Police HQ. The new subway stations are dotted around a few locations in lower city (you can check the subway map for other locations once you find one), and the you can catch the over ground train from the train station building near Tech Bleu area.

Next update I am planning to add a more descriptive map to the game so that all the new things will be easier to find!

reviews.review_description: An immersive and stylish cyberpunk sandbox with strong Blade Runner vibes. Flying through the city feels fantastic and the atmosphere is absolutely incredible.

developer_response.body: Thanks Majerik! This pretty much sums up everything I was aiming for when I started out making the game, so it's really great to hear!

reviews.review_description: Only game on vr that did cyberpunk right. Hope they keep adding and patching for years to come.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much!

I'm a big fan of all things cyberpunk (especially 80's/90's stuff!), so it really is a labour of love creating this world 🙂 We've still got of a lot of stuff in the works for more updates, plus a ton more ideas for the future, so we hope to carry on building for a good while yet!

reviews.review_description: Very well done!!! I just found out about this and just wow

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! We're not so visible on the store, so it's great to hear when new players discover the game 🙂

reviews.review_description: Flying in a police futuristic vehicle is fun and the graphics are clean and smooth. Great job!

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks so much for the review! We really appreciate it :)

reviews.review_description: I don’t think I’ve been so infatuated with a low budget vr title before this one, the game has way more depth than initially perceived and becomes immersive and moorish to the point I am now an alcoholic mcpd officer who is highly against synths, but that’s not the only way you can play, games amazing and cannot praise it enough as it comes out of early access soon and the devs and community are some of the best, I wish them luck on this project.

developer_response.body: Thanks Poised! You certainly know how to lean in to the dystopian side of Metacity 😂 Maybe one day you will find it in your heart to help out a synthetic in need, but I doubt it! 🙌

reviews.review_description:
Normally, I don't give any reviews until I've 100% completed a game but with one, I'll make an exception. The main reason is that I'm fully aware that this project just started off as a single dev which is now two with a few of other folks sometimes chipping in. What they've achieved thus far for such a small dev team is quite extraordinary, really. I rarely give 5 star reviews as that means in my eyes that a game has to be close to perfect but I give the dev 5 star for their effort so far.

In the short time I played, I whizzed around the cyber cityscape in my hover car, messing around with the controls and admiring the scenery. I screwed around with my cockpit buttons, radio station and seat position. I decided I liked the windscreen reflection effect so left it on.  I visited the police HQ. One of the female police operatives kept giving me the eye. I completed a few basic missions, shot a few skin jobs. I went off the grid for a bit, ignoring the missions, visiting a church, listened to a cyborg giving a sermon at the alter, admired the decor, cyber murals and stain glass. I went to a noodle bar, paid 3 credits for meal. I found a nightclub, paid 20 credits for an entry ticket, and saw about 100 gyrating bodies on the dance floor and suddenly got a flashback from the first Matrix film when Neo visits the club. Took a cocktail from the waitress who was doing circuits around the club. Found a place where I could buy my own apartment but didn't have enough credits (bummer). Found a game cartridge for the home game console (yep, I saw the recent patch updste dev). Decided to do another mission and visited a movie theatre to meet a contact while Metropolis was playing on the screen.

In essence, what I'm trying to say here is that I think it's great what such a small team has achieved so far with all the little details. Is it perfect right now? Of course not, but I can see the huge potential and I'm intrigued to see what the team will add in the future. Show them some love, buy the game for it's modest price, make their lives a little easier so they can all quit their regular day jobs and focus on this (assuming that they're not already fully dedicated to this). More sales = more games updates and no, I don't earn commission or the know the team personally.

One little suggestion to devs. I like all the different looking NPCs wandering around the streets with a fair amount of cosmetic variety. I think this may have been mentioned before but the ability to interact with some of them would be a nice touch. Also, I notice that they just walk through each other rather than avoid collisions. I know that with so many NPCs in some areas, this may be hard to execute. Maybe, some time in the future, NPC conversations can be handled with AI so they appear more natural and dynamic. Perhaps that's beyond mobile hardware capabilities at this stage. 🤔 

I shall update my review at some point in the future as this game gradually develops and when I get the chance to dip in and out of the Metacity. 🫡

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review! 😊 I've actually been working a lot recently on a new NPC system which should make the crowds appear much smarter, so this is something that should be arriving in an update soon!

There's so many plans that we have for the game, choosing what elements to work on next is always a challenge, but every day the game makes little jumps forwards! We always want to keep expanding the world, but also revisit existing parts and make improvements where possible. It's a never ending process, but we love to do it!

Thanks again for taking the time to leave such kind words and feedback. It really means a lot to us!

reviews.review_title: Updated Review for a Great Game
reviews.review_description: Very pleasantly surprised with the amount of content/locations which have been added to the game since I first looked at it (probably 18 months ago) and have enjoyed my time inside the game so far (now rank 5), only one thing has caused me a bit of confusion, when interviewing suspects in the interview room, the big red eye scanner doesn't really seem to provide much of an indication regarding the suspect and I found it difficult to notice any genuine difference in reaction.  Having now recorded 5 full episodes for my YouTube channel, I have to say that I am still finding new things to do and see, which is frankly amazing for a game with a very simple game-loop, the life on the streets puts AAA studios to shame and I cannot wait for my first game of Rollerball when I'm next off-duty (although it is very loud inside the arena!)

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks so much for the review!

The interrogation can be tricky to start with. Humans will show no reaction to evidence, synthetics will start to blink faster depending on the seriousness of the evidence placed in front of them (and higher level evidence will make their eyes flash on the monitor), you can stack up multiple evidence to get a stronger reaction 🙂

I hope you have fun with the Rollerb... ahem, I mean Velocity Ball! Looking forward to watching some of the YouTube vids! 😊

A new update should be landing soon!

reviews.review_description: So this game is great if your thinking of buying it do it  thank you devs for making this game

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for buying it! (and even more for reviewing it 😊)

We're working hard on the next updates right now!

reviews.review_title: Incredible 1 of 1 Vibe - Best Quest GAME
reviews.review_description:
A steal for the price, $10 for the BEST VR GAME ON THE MARKET???? What are you people doing how is this not 5 star? If anyone leaves a non-5 star review it has to be fake to from competitors as this game is 10 out of 5 stars. 

Feels like you are actually in a city, part of the city, the flying is so smooth.

I just found myself sitting at the movie theatre for 10 minutes just watching. Just in awe I feel immersed

The best current game out for VR. THANK YOU TO THE CREATORS! Killing it!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much!

Of course, everyone has their own favourite ways of playing, and maybe some others expect something different from the game, but I'm so happy that most people really understand what we're trying to create! It was always important for me to create a world that could be lived in outside the gameplay loop, so it's great to hear from players who enjoy just hanging around in the city in addition to completing missions!

Anyway, we continue to add new ideas, expand the world and listen to feedback for future updates, so that eventually we can average a 'perfect 5' 😁

reviews.review_description: I can tell the Devs are passionate about bringing a Cyberpunk universe to VR. One thing lets this title down however, a heavy reliance on AI. This title has great potential but the use of AI will turn people away. I see the vision that the devs are working towards but the reliance on AI is unacceptable. Instead of AI voices for dispatch, radio personalities and phone voices please hire amateur voice actors, we are begging for voice roles in anything we can get. Instead of AI generated songs on the radio stations use copyright free synthwave tracks made by humans. This is an excellent opportunity to give amateur voice actors and musicians a chance to shine. To the devs, please go to amateur artists instead of AI, we can breath a life and soul into your world that AI can never replace

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review!

I completely get the comments about AI, the two of us making the game are also artists and musicians. We are looking at working a bit more with other musicians for things like the radio stations, but for other parts of the game it is a little more complex.

The use of AI came about, in part, from the pretty unconventional development process for the game (my fault!). I didn't have a background in game development at all before Metacity, I was just a Quest 1 owner who thought it would be cool to have a flying car game! I learned C#, Unity and 3D modelling to 'just enough' of a level to start creating the game in the few hours of time I had free outside of my day job.

It has led, I would say, to a very unorthodox approach to designing the game, with lots of mini-projects on the go at once, momentum deciding which part of the game is worked on in my next few hours of spare time. Basically, I am making the game up as I go along!

Some ideas might be worked on for weeks/months and then get binned, or entire parts of the game get re-written completely based on some small new idea, It's a glorious mess of sorts, but one I think would be frustrating for outside collaborators to endure!

It's not impossible for me to bring in outside help for things like voice acting of course, it's just that it requires a level of co-ordination and forward-planning that  isn't there yet. I would really like to improve on this in the future, but right now, the priority is to get the game 'finished' (design wise) with the help of AI. Once I have a complete design of the game, I would truly love to revisit lots of the music, art and dialogue, with content made by myself, as well as with other human contributors, if there is an appetite for that.

So I think all of your comments are valid, and I don't expect any of my explanation above to absolve me of any of those criticisms! I'm just hoping to explain that this game is made with love, hard work, AI, and a developer who doesn't really know what he's doing!

reviews.review_description: I honestly think this game is a REAL VR gem and deserves way more attention and support!!! . If you’re looking for a casual, easy-to-pick-up standalone experience with cyberpunk/Blade Runner vibes and stylish visuals, this definitely scratches that itch! It’s updated regularly, and the developers are very active on Discord. Ideas for the game are very welcomed and appreciated. There’s a surprising amount of replayability and so much to explore that I felt it was worth giving it a shout-out in case anyone missed it. The more you get used to it, level up, and upgrade , the better this game gets . This game is something really special and the devs have a lot of passion for their creation here. I respect this and will support them all the way! I don’t write many reviews, but this game deserves it. Keep up the great work! It doesn’t go unnoticed. Highly recommend!

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks so much for the review. We really appreciate it!

We're just trying to build our dream game (and cram in as many ideas as possible!). So it's great to hear when people discover the game and love it 🙂

We're still working as hard as ever on new updates, so thanks for the support!

reviews.review_description:
Awesome residence, with an interactive game console and a text based PC game to keep you busy. And refreshments.

I like the controls for the car, quest controllers attached with cables like a CB but it makes me realize I’d rather have a stick or a wheel for the car and an actual radio for answering calls. Or like a loudspeaker to play with.

It says there’s a story in the mode I’m playing, but it seems to be a bunch of fetch quests on a never ending loop. I feel like I’ve gone as far as the game will let me but there’s a bunch of bobble heads and game cartridges still locked and unavailable. I also can’t see how to play the games I bought. Where do I get the console?

The car chases get kind of frustrating later, when the cars are fast and handle well. I can keep pace with them on the tight routes but hitting them feels impossible sometimes.  Sports cars should be fragile if they’re that fast and handle so tight. Or the challenge might be right but the payout is wrong. Those missions are hard.

This is a cool game with an addictive gameplay loop. The car is fun to drive and the city is a good environment to drive it in.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review!

The story missions will start to play out once you have collected 10 illegal weapons (from neutralising armed synthetics) and also when you have returned 10 broken cameras to Police HQ (at some point you should get a mission asking you to find a broken camera, after collecting the first camera, other broken ones will appear on your HUD when nearby).

To get the console to play the games, you must own one of the properties that has a console as part of the furniture, there is one purchasable property in Residential (next to the landing area) and another in Tech Zone.

For the car chases, make sure you have the siren on to give you an extra speed boost, this should give you an edge over the faster cars :) 

We're always listening to feedback to help improve the game in future updates, so thanks again. If you need any help progressing in the game, check out our Discord, there are a lot of experienced Patrols hanging out on there!

reviews.review_description:
It's a pretty cool game. Not really sure what I am doing most of the time but I like flying about. 

Some suggestions 
Id like an auto pilot to get me to the next objective please. 

I have a bhaptics jacket and on the demo app for it has a rain affect be, really cool to have bhaptics included.

Maybe have the flight controllers look something different to actual meta controllers. 

😊

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review and the feedback!

Autopilot is an interesting idea💡, I shall have a think about how I can make that work!

If you ever need any help finding your way through Metacity, check out our Discord, there's a lot a helpful Patrollers ready to help with tips on the game!

reviews.review_description: If you understand nightride vibes then this game is a no brainer. A must own. If you are looking for GTA or PVP PVE then you come to the wrong place. This is about endless dreary rainy nights, cruising around the neon lit Blade Runner inspired city as droplets run down your windshield , patrolling in between calls from HQ to neutralize rogue skinjobs, pick up and deliver important evidence, confiscate illegal guns, flying vehicle chases with your lights and siren blazing...(other stuff too,  I am not deep enough in the game yet.) Then there's the off duty time where you grab some ramen at a street food vendor, purchase upgrades, take in a movie, hang out at the club and knock back a few cocktails. Time to hit the sack, wake up to some much needed coffee and a cigarette before punching in for another days work. This small dev team delivers some of the best flight mechanics, smooth frames, synthwave tunes, an absorbing immersive atmosphere, and an entire city to cruise around where you can actually touch down in an almost seamless way to the bustling pedestrian filled streets and alleyway locations in various districts below.  It's a world where you are free to roleplay and live out your electric dreams.  Bravo to this dev team who are more passionate than most on this store. This game is a love letter

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! Yes, you get it! 🙂

Living the life of a Blade Runner, surrounded by all that atmosphere was what drove me to start making the game. It's a world that we truly love creating and improving, so it's always great to hear from players who are on board with our dystopian dream!

We have many more plans for Metacity, I really hope you have fun discovering more of it!

reviews.review_description:
This game might be the product of a really small team, but it manages to capture the Blade Runner -feeling like nothing I have seen before.

The missions might feel somewhat basic but flying around the dystopian neon light city never gets old. I also love the fact that the devs have added so many random places like a night club and a theater.

I hope that a lot of people will find this absolute gem so that you can make the city even more detailed 🙏

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much!

We're slowly adding more missions with some more complexity, I wanted to make a game that could also be played a little more casually in short bursts, so I didn't want the missions to be become too overblown, but its a balance that I'm always trying to perfect!

We keep listening to feedback and are still working as hard as ever on improving and expanding the game. There'll be more to come for sure!

reviews.review_description:
Please keep sending updates this game is truly amazing.

Bravo to the Creators, out of all the games i have, the only one i play is this one.

developer_response.body:
Hey thanks so much! We really appreciate it 🙂

A new update will be landing very soon, and we're working on plenty more for the future too. Lots more plans for Metacity! 😉

reviews.review_description:
It may be weird at first, especially with the retro graphics and the fact that the tutorial shows you some basic stuff that is not enough, but once you pass more time and start discovering it gets so much fun. The different mission modes, the areas with the hidden spots make the game far more interesting than it looks. The real highlight is the flying car with the excellent dynamics. 
However, I am not sure whether there is a story as you just go on with the missions and it doesn't feel like it is meaningful.

Some things that I think that need fixing: 

- Better display and use of the map while in the car with the missions and the areas
- Have a map while walking to an area
- Have a menu with the menu button (inactive) instead of putting all the options in the car.
- Unlock upgrades with each rank (especially weapons) and not from the beginning. Also, you gain way much credits and it is easy for every upgrade.
- Armed synthetics run in crowded places and it is impossible not to lose credit when they shoot. 
- Introduce time for day/night and needs (going to sleep), introduce limited ammunition.
- Put hospital in a separate area
- Being able to pass through physical obstacles (bug?) + difficult to pick a dropped item.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and all of the feedback!

I know that I have created a bit of a weird game, I wanted to create a feeling of being planted inside something like the old 8/16-bit games I grew up with, with a more casual experience than most VR games of this type. 

I also wanted to encourage exploration and discovery, rather than a linear experience. It does make the game quite niche, but I'm always listening out for feedback to help make it more accessible :) 

There are some story elements that emerge once enough armed synthetics have been neutralized, and also once enough broken cameras have been returned. It's quite a limited story so far, but I'm building on it with each update! Also there are some things that unlock according to progress (such as more weapons and properties), there's more to discover beyond what is available at the beginning ;)

A lot of the suggestions on your list are things on my to-do list, I'm constantly making tweaks based on suggestions from the community, so thanks again for the feedback, it's truly appreciated!

reviews.review_description: I bought this game back in March 25 - such a great game with potential that could (should?) put the willies up these bigger developers.  The flight experience is how I imagined Blade Runner would be!  Can I ask the Devs, has there been any updates since March 2025?

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review!

Yes, there's been a few new things since March! 

We added: Melee weapons, dojo, shoulder storage pockets, a couple more properties,  gang hideout missions (in a re-designed Building Zone),  informer missions, POI markers, upper city traffic, playable video games, multi language support, plus a new story mission for the Clippers' plot line!

And there is a lot more to come. We're working on the next updates right now! 🙂

reviews.review_description: Almost immediately gave up on it because at first you can't see below the dashboard of your car. Glad I stuck with it, you get floor glass as an upgrade. It reminds me of the time when imagination was an important part of the gaming experience. You're left to fill in a lot of blanks and it's great.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review! I think your comment about filling in the blanks and using your imagination is spot on, it's something I love a lot about old school gaming, and that is the kind of feeling I really wanted to try and create.

It's a fine balance to prevent the game from just becoming totally confusing of course (or just having players missing out on big sections of the game), but it's something I'm always tweaking!

reviews.review_description: Its a tech demo. Cool for the price im sure its a lot for one dude. Alot of ai assets. Restarted ai...cool world. Its worth just driving and flying around, everything else is half baked. But for the price I say grab it for the experience.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

I know that I've created a bit of a niche game, in that I aimed for a more casual open-ended experience. There is a lot more to discover in the game beyond the initial gameplay loop for those that stick with it and explore, but I know that this approach certainly does not suit everyone.

Of course, there's plenty of improvements that can still be made, I keep working on the game every spare minute I have, and there are loads more plans and updates in the works. Feedback really helps drive the development of new updates, so thanks again!

reviews.review_title: Been playing it over a year
reviews.review_description: my top 2 fav game on quest and i own most titles. The dev has produced the type of game we ALL have wanted but never had. Its not finished, but you will feel emersed. The soundtrack and song selections help a lot and really rock the 80s cyber synth feel. Get it!

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review! It's great to hear from players who keep coming back to Metacity 🙂

Still lots of work to do, but we are full of ideas and working hard at it everyday! New updates soon!

reviews.review_description:
Im talking about the blueprint to one of the greatest games touch the meta market... this game concept is great. Layout is great. THE CAR AND THE RADIO?MANNNN the small things to do like the ideas of A MOVIE IN THE THEATRE AND DRINKING AT THE CLUB DANCING AND ALL THAT... WOW DEVS... WOW.. THE LITTLE THINGS.... Love the potential it's showing and how I haven't even gotten that deep into it yet....... BUT.... somehow this still seems a bit underwhelming and I thinks it's from the repetitive simplified 1-2 step missions and the easy enemies that's you barely chase.. the dull and dead NPCs. ADDING EFFECTD TO SMOKING AND DRINKING TOO.. LIKE BLUR IT OR SOMETHING... AND COMBAT... PLEASE MAKE IT FUN... the gun popping up with grip is odd too. Add a belt maybe a car inventory. MORE MUSIC... FRESH 84 AND THE OTHERS (SOUR CAKE) BEAUTIFUL... BUT need more.. 

(3 BIGGEST THINGS THAT TAKE AWAY FROM THE EXPERIENCE)

1. underwhelming simple missions (which just may be cause im just starting out im getting into it tho)

2. Combat is way to simple... grab gun. Find dude. Shoot. Mission complete... maybe give better scenarios instead of them just flailing around the city.. let us punch..

3 DULL NPC and AI system.. tho it's so good to see a game like this.. it sucks to see the potential not being implemented in the BASE of the game and what's going on around you. 


This game has potential devs and I'm going to continue to support it as long as you guys do.. it definitely is amazing for the price and impressive for camp size.. you guys are amazing and im not saying the game is bad... it's just that this could absolutely be THEEEE game... dont cut yourselves short

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review and for all of the feedback!

The two of us are working every day on building and improving the world and gameplay. It's a pretty big task for us, but we love every minute of it, and have many future plans!

When I first started creating the game, the aim was for a very casual experience, and more about the atmosphere of exploring the city. As things have progressed, I've added more 'action' to the the game, but there are still remnants of the more casual aspect that have been retained (I don't want to lose these entirely). It's a balance that I'm always looking to perfect! 

In all honesty, I never expected players would stick around in the game for as long as they do, so a lot of my efforts recently have been to add more progression in to the game. After the next couple of updates, I plan to go back and improve a lot of the core game to bring the game world a lot more to life.

Making this game has been, and continues to be a massive learning experience for me (I literally had to learn to code to make this game! 😂), and the feedback from the community has been a massive help in bringing this game to life, so thanks again, we'll continue building and tweaking to perfect the game of our dreams!

reviews.review_description: Not going to lie, but it got to me at first. Just something you may need to acclimate to like flying a plane. Love being able to fly through the air from point to point. Plus playing bumper cars in the sky lol. Recommended for the strong players that can stomach it...

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! Yes, I know the flying can be hard to stomach for some players, I added a few comfort options to try and help, and playing sitting down helps a lot of people. I'm so glad that you persevered and can enjoy it now!

reviews.review_title: Four stars is a little generous but
reviews.review_description:
It's a remarkable little game. The free roaming flying car map is beautiful and soothing.
There is jank here and there but the atmosphere is great.
I wouldn't say there is a huge story (which I don't miss tbh) and gameplay sections on the ground are pretty basic and more like mini games.

I'd say there are other titles out there at this price that are better games for sure, but at the same time none of those offer what this does (cruising around at your leisure in a hover cop car doing routine police work in the far future).

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

I think what you say is very fair. I originally set out to make this game more about the atmosphere than the gameplay (though of course there has to be some gameplay in there!). 

My thoughts were that there are already a lot of great games out there that do FPS, gunplay and physics really well, but not so many that you can just play casually at your own pace and experience the atmosphere (which I think is a better fit for this type of game world). 

I'm glad you're enjoying what is in there so far, It's a slow-burner for sure, though over time, there are more secrets to discover for those who wish to go and discover them. And there will be more to come!

reviews.review_description: Love this... I play mainly pcvr and this is up there , as a standalone , amazing... Smooth movement, good FPS... Only just started playing but already worth admission... Keep up the great work developer's... Amazing

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks so much for the review! It really means a lot to us.

It's great to hear that you're loving it so far! We're still working hard on updates for the future, we have many more plans!

reviews.review_description: This game's awesome, it's got that cool retro feel mixed with a cyberpunk future. It starts easy, then gets more complex at a good speed. You can totally get lost in it, just chillin' in the sky with some great music, or go after the bad guys and move the story along. Definitely a 10/10 VR game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review!

For me, the game was always about creating that retro cyberpunk vibe. So I'm glad you can feel it in there :)

We're still working on adding much more to the game. I hope you'll love what's coming next!

reviews.review_description:
Aside from its Blade Runner vibes, the atmosphere also reminds me somewhat of the first Deus Ex (2000) in a good way. The flying mechanics are convincing. The city is also appealing, regardless of the AI ​​assistance.

The small development team seems very talented, and I'm very curious to see if and how the game develops further.

Bug ?
In passenger missions, it sometimes happens that either the pickup point or the destination is inaccessible within a large building complex, making the mission impossible to complete. Have I missed something?

PS:
Thanks for the replies. I'm relieved it's not a bug!
Even after several hours of gameplay, the game still has its charm. Just standing on the roof of the police station and soaking up the atmosphere, or relaxing and flying over the city with Radio 103FM (or something like that) – that's something special. And that's why I'm very curious to see what else you have planned.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review! A few people have mentioned similarities with Deus EX, it's not a game I ever played, but I can see what you mean! 🙂

I think the issue you see with the passenger pickup is that the pickup/drop off point is inside the sewer. There are two entrances to the sewer marked with red lights in the upper city. If you start a race in Industrial, that course passes through the sewer, so you'll get an idea of where the entrances are for when you need them for passenger missions.

We're still working on the next updates right now. I hope you'll like what's coming next!

reviews.review_title: Best Replacement for AirCar on Steam
reviews.review_description:
I was looking for an extended version of Aircar on PC/Steam, but it wasn't available, so I ended up with Meta Patrol.
Really well done for a mobile game.
The only thing I find disappointing is that you can't really look down while flying; you have to tilt the Aircar downwards. It's not a big deal, but it doesn't stay in this position. As soon as you let go of the stick, it springs back to the vertical position. It would be nice if the windows were a bit lower so you could see more of the city. But otherwise, great job!

developer_response.body:
Hey, Thanks so much for the review!

You can actually turn of the auto-stabilize in the 'Aircar Settings' menu on the car dashboard. Also, you can purchase windows for the car doors and floor in the garage at HQ, which let you see more of the city below 😊

Hope you have fun exploring Metacity!

reviews.review_description:
I've been waiting for a game like this for forever. A blade runner type game where I can fly around the city and walk through the streets. 

From first experiencing the Androids Dream cardboard app and the Blade runner Replicant Pursuit on the Gear VR I always wanted to control the flying and get out of the car. This game allows me to do that and I'm so in awe of it. It's very much similar in ways to GTA. Free roam, choose your missions, buy properties and choose which radio station suits your vibe whilst flying your vehicle. 

You can upgrade you're car, your apartment, your weapons and you can enter theatres, night clubs and basically have free roam of the entire city. The sounds, music and atmosphere are all so perfect it sets the mood for the game. You can also select which radio station you like to hear whilst flying around and adjust your seat for a better view.

Well done guys! You've done a great job on this and I will be spending a lot more time Policing the skies of Meta City. 🚨

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much! 'GTA meets Blade Runner' was my ambition when starting to develop the game, so your references make me happy :)

We keep adding more to discover with every update. I hope you keep enjoying life on patrol!

reviews.review_description:
Every so often a game arrives that makes you realise just how good V.R is, this is one of them. I've been playing V.R since the launch of the Quest 1 and whilst I'm a huge fan of the medium in general, my usage has wained in recent months. I still go back to favourites but little new has excited me. Metacity Patrol has been on my watch list for a year probably, I saw it on sale and jumped in. Sorry devs, it genuinely feels wrong buying this game on sale given how good it is. 

This game has absolutely blown my mind. The mix of Blade Runner, Robocop, eighties action films in general, RPG, and light life sim is staggering. This is the closest we have to GTA, but honestly I prefer this much more. This city is alive, breathing, vast and awaiting your discovery. 

I love finding new areas, streets, corners and surprises. Upgrading your car not only boosts performance but alters it aesthetically and I love that. Upgrading your home, your gun. Interviewing synthetics, smoking a vape that actually emits smoke is insane. 

The sound design is equally excellent with five or six different radio stations to choose from, we could use a few extra tracks though. It runs brilliantly. I'm stunned by home smooth everything is given the scale. Getting a call over the radio, punching the siren as a deep synthwave bass drops on the radio feels like the beginning of a movie, everytime. I genuinely get a rush from that. You feel like such a bad-a**

This in my opinion is an advert for purchasing a Quest 3. I truly hope they add an arcade with working games, a video store to peruse and a game shop with carts you can purchase to play on your home console, yes, you can play games, at your in game home.

I've fallen in love, and every night after my actual work finishes,  I clock on and begin another shift in the M.C. If they could somehow implement multi player it would blow my mind. I want to explore the city with a friend. Maybe just add a ride along feature. Ultimately I just want to thank the developers and all involved in this gift.

developer_response.body:
Wow, thanks so much for the review! We've tried hard to cram in as many ideas as possible to make the game world feel alive, and we're not stopping yet! 😊 An arcade and video game store are both being worked on, plus a ton of other Metacity stuff! (and more story of course). 

It's so great to hear from people discovering the game, it really makes all the long hours worth it! So thanks again! 😁

reviews.review_description: I am blown tf away...this is probably the biggest hidden gem ever created i even gifted to a homie that was also blown away. This is an updated review..I had suggestions for what I'd like to see and after a few hours all that stuff was in the game xD so much love and support for this dev it hurts. Something big is being created under everyone's noses and yal need to lock in. Collect your unauthorized weapons and broken cameras to start doing cop/criminal missions and unlock new areas this game simply doesn't disappoint. From fighting crime to decking out my skills and vehicle to playing mini games to just exploring the map smoking joints and enjoying the view from high up sipping a beer after a long shift the atmosphere is absolutely insane. I'm stoked for the current build and the future of this game. If your think about picking it up this is our cyberpunk guys. And it's actually amazing. I'd kiss this dev if I could. I've heard different things I've heard it's one guy and I've also heard it's a small team. But I wanna give a big thanks to whoever was involved in creating this masterpiece. What a great game

developer_response.body:
Hey thanks so much for the review update! I really appreciate it :)

We're a team of two building this game (I started out by myself, then teamed up with a 3D artist after a few months), with contributions from a few friends here and there. We love creating this world, and have so many more ideas for it in the future! 

The next updates are being worked on right now, there will be more to come early in the new year! 😘

reviews.review_title: This is THE hidden gem of Meta Quest.
reviews.review_description:
Combine Judge Dredd, Edgerunners, Tron, and Blade Runner, and you have this incredible game.

There is much to explore, waaaaaaay more than the POIs on the HUD.

Do yourself a service and play the game and explore.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks so much for the review! It's always great to hear that we're doing something right! 😊

reviews.review_description:
I think with a little more work and a few changes this game could really be a winner.  I enjoyed the flying car after I got used to controlling it.  I like the darkness, the constant rain and the "Blade Runner" feel.  I have finished several assignments but found there were a few that was hard due to lack of instructions but easy once you figure out what you have to do or where to go. 
UPDATE:  I have played this game every night since I first reviewed it here and have gotten further into the game.  Most of the items I mentioned needed work, turns out were there already in the game, you just had to get further into it to unlock/upgrade the guns, cars & the H.U.D.   The further I get into the game the more I am enjoying it.  Last night I went up against a gang of bad guys in a building and had to battle my way through it. VERY COOL.  It still needs some work, but this game gets much more interesting as you progress in the game. I got to go to new locations that I have never been in, and I took time to go "off duty" to just explore and check out the "Points of Interests" which allowed me to upgrade my car & guns.  I bumped my rating up from a three to a four and I could see it going to a five in the future.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review and the feedback!

I wanted to add an element of exploration to the game without making the game too linear. I still need to perfect the balance of discovery with enough guidance so players know what they should be doing to progress. There is a 'POI' option for the HUD that should give the locations of things like missions objectives even when they are far away (also this will show the location of hospital etc. when nearby), perhaps you have this deactivated? Either way, I will keep making tweaks to make things a little more obvious :)

Crimes occur randomly around Metacity, so there is the potential for them to interrupt other activities you might be doing at the time, this is why I added the option to go 'Off Duty', or also to reject missions when they come in, but I get that it still might be a distraction.

Anyway, I keep working every day on improving and expanding the game, so all feedback is welcome. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: Need more robots like different type of robots around the city like serving robots cleaning robots

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're actually working on more robots (amongst a whole load of other stuff!) right now 😁

reviews.review_description:
Rated a 4 because the tutorial was VERY minimal, don't know if you can bend down, dropped my baton and could get it, driving makes my stomach turn, sitting in car can't really see the city, had to adjust to where my head was out the roof lol I REALLY HOPE THEY DO A ROAD MAP and keep up the assignments. Also, Idk if you can just walk a beat.

Also couldn't sit to interrogate or knew what to do once I put evidence on table.. 

More guidance is needed IMO

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and the feedback!

You can sit/crouch by pressing the right thumbstick. Some more of the controls are described on the screens on the desk at Police HQ, but yes, I should probably add some more info to the tutorial/early steps. I intended for a lot of the mechanics to be discovered naturally in the game rather than have a lengthy tutorial, but possibly I need to balance this a bit better.

We're always listening out for feedback, it really helps us make improvements to the game, so thanks again!

reviews.review_description:
Definitely the potential to be the BEST game on VR !!!   So much Fun.   Im already addicted.  I must play when everyone goes to sleep 😄 🤣 

Also this game was MADE to pair up with the WOOJER haptic VEST 4.  Wow!!!!!!  Amazing

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We're still working hard on the next updates, and have many more plans for Metacity! I'm kinda addicted to expanding and improving the game, I have to work on it when everyone goes to sleep 😂

reviews.review_description:
This game is great! The atmosphere is so cool!
Like being in a blade runner style movie.

You start off at the Police station, get some basic tutorial tasks and then you are let free.
I like the idea with the hover car, how the descends down into the lower levels of the city to explore and take on missions.
The car radio you can switch on with various stations. 
Even has a siren complete with flashing red/blue lights!
It gives up options to adjust your seating position too, which is nice.
The car is very easy to control… may give some people motion sickness though.

There’s plenty of areas to explore around the city… you can even enter a sports event (which reminds me of Rollerball)
And you can buy your own property too!

Some missions you get can be collect evidence ones or even chasing suspect and gunning them down ect..

At the station you can buy upgrades for your character and car, to help with tracking, shooting ect…

There’s also arcade games to be found which you can play on that have high scores.

The dev is very active with this one and adds new content regularly aswell :)

It gives you a great feel with the future vibe and impresses with what the dev has done.

Go live out your future cop dreams!!

reviews.review_description: Seen this on YouTube by a well known person who howls to his wolfpack and instantly thought wow, looks pretty sick, as already a fan of flight sims and space sims I thought id buy this and give it a whirl, and let me say out of all my games (which is many) ive spent all my time on this, playing on standalone quest 2 and the graphics are sweet,  being able to fly  your vehicle across the skyline, the different areas you can go to. Hey, thanks for the review! I know we've spoken on Discord already, but I reply here too anyway😊

reviews.review_description:

Well worth the money 
Kudos to the guys who made this 
Can’t wait for more updates
F Y I 
If u ever decide to launch a DLC I would definitely pay for it.

reviews.review_description:
Update: thank you to the developer for responding to my review. I can totally understand the reasoning behind having to simplify things and the certain systems to work on the Quest efficiently. I can appreciate what was done and I did not return it. With such a small team I want to support smaller developers. I just hope that the game will continue to improve and it’s not abandoned because I do like what’s here. 

====================================

This is a good Blade Runner style cop game. It seems to have a decent amount of depth, even though I’m not really far into it yet. 

The graphics while flying around the city sell the immersion pretty well, and the gunplay is not too bad either. 

My issues come in when you are walking around cities. Characters clip into each other and seem to just have one walking pattern total for every one in the population. There is never a time where you find somebody that’s moving differently from any other NPC. This is extremely immersion breaking, and the graphics of the actual character models are really subpar too.

Lastly, my main gripe is that the missions seem to repeat way too often. I literally took four jobs in a row, and they were all exactly the same. Speaking of this particular mission it is one where I have to subdue a criminal. In every one of these, it’s just a character running around like crazy amongst all the other NPCs. There is no challenge at all. You just look for the guy that’s running crazy fast among all the other people and shoot him. Not compelling at all TBH. 

Those issues aside, I do know that this game was developed by two people, and don’t get me wrong. I am not a developer at all, and I understand how hard it must be to develop a game of the scale on the Meta quest with such a small team. 

That being said, I think it would be a disservice to the community and to the game developer if I didn’t voice my opinion to make the game better. 

I’m really hoping the game will continue to improve and I will update my review once the content is a little more fleshed out. 

For the price, it’s not bad, but with all of the raving reviews, I figured I would bring people down to earth a little bit more with their expectations before picking this one up.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review and all the great feedback! 

All the points you make are fair. There are of course a lot of compromises I had to make designing the game. For the atmosphere I wanted to create, I needed to have large populations of NPCs in some areas, and this meant that we really had to 'dumb down' their complexity (both in appearance and physics) to make it possible on Quest, but I'm constantly trying to find ways to improve things in that area! 

My vision for the game (still to be completely realised!) is for an atmospheric/exploratory experience with moments of action, which together uncovers a loose narrative over time. The gameplay loop is semi-randomised with a mixture of core missions and plot-progressing missions which are introduced gradually. It probably makes the game a little more niche than expected (and repetitive when the random mission generator spawns a wave of similar missions), and in parts the game does not hit the mark of my ambitions yet, but we're working further towards it every day, and feedback from the community certainly helps us direct our efforts for improving the experience.

So thanks again for taking the time to write your feedback, we will keep working hard building the game of our dreams!

reviews.review_description: This game is just brilliant for being so early in development. Honestly this game is so in depth it’s crazy. It has probably the largest map out of any VR game I’ve played and it’s so open world. The controls are like no other game and for how different they are they were surprisingly easy to learn. And in my opinion the best feature this game has is that the creator is so open to their community I have seen them talking to plenty of players answering questions and even greeting most people personally on the discord server. Overall this game has some amazing potential and if it keeps growing at the rate it has been it just might be my favorite game on VR.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! It's great that we have a community that is as enthusiastic about the game as we are, it really inspires us to keep working on adding more to the game! We'll continue to keep going until we deliver your favourite VR game! 😊

reviews.review_description:
The atmosphere, the music and the flying car are all well done and the city is beautiful. The game is more of an experience to relax, explore and do small tasks to be entertained. I have some suggestions for the developers.

- A faster story progression. I've spent more than ten hours doing nothing but missions, but the plot development is too slow. 
- More complex and unique missions. It becomes tiresome to do the same missions jumping in and out of the car constantly.
- A more mature tone, more action, blood, suspense, thrill, etc.
- Better NPCs (behavior, graphics, interactions, movements, etc.)
- Being able to grab the gun from my waist or belt and do manual loading.
- Guns installed outside the air car (maybe as an upgrade).
- The locations need a lot more details, for example the phones were excellent additions.
- The moon doesn't look good, it's too big and pixelated.
- Real asian writing.

I really like this game, but I'm getting tired after ten hours of doing the same things with little progress.

Metacity has a lot of potential.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review and the feedback! A lot of the feedback is stuff that I plan to work on for sure. I certainly want to make the earlier stages of the game more interesting with less grind. I chose to make most of the incidental writing illegible so that the repetition of the textures did not become too obvious (resources are very limited for such a large world!), but all feedback is useful to us and we listen to it all 🙂 We'll keep working on improving and expanding as much as we can, we have lots of plans still to realise in Metacity!

reviews.review_title: The best experience in VR of my lifetime
reviews.review_description:
Update: since release.
OMG! it got better and yes im forever greatful. Living the dream, man😏


Let me start by saying this, this is the kind of game and experience that I have been waiting decades for in my life. No console game no CPU game and no VR game has made me feel like this has. What I mean to say is if I haven't made it clear yet this is exactly the kind of thing I have dreamed about since I was 10, 15, and 20 years old and Beyond! I'll just say it I'm well into my adult years and what the developers have in their hands is everything that I could imagine. I've escaped not just my life but into a new life!! The world feels present to me and I feel present in it. What I mean by that is they've captured the essence and scope of daily life from the mundane to the exciting. For instance should I buy the popcorn or the cigarette while I watch my movie in a movie theater then I flew to in my car at a futuristic city or should I swing by my apartment that not only can I furnish but already comes with an outdoor glass in case fish pond accompanied by wind chimes and a beautiful tree on top of the skyscraper I just flew to and part my flying car, which by the way is a police car because I'm in officer in this new life. There's so much to say and unpack here with where this game or what I would like to call it experience has been created. all I can say at this point is everything that has been put into place already shows the Magnificent Direction which is obviously the right direction my future Favorite place to be is headed. The time I have spent which has not been very long has made me have hope not only in VR but also a place for my life that brings Joy, which I know I will spend much time experiencing within the universe that is being created here! I do plan on updating my comments with further experiences and as things progress to express my gratitude and happiness that this exists and can only get better from here. Thank you Developers for making my dreams come true too! I needed this, no fooling!!:-)

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! It really means a lot, and we never get tired of hearing from people who love the game :) When I started developing this, it was really designed as a world for me to escape to, and even as the game expanded and developed, I have always kept this feeling in mind. I'm so glad that you're enjoying it so far, we have so much planned for this game, and reading reviews like yours gives us more motivation than ever to keep working on building our dream game!

reviews.review_description:
Man y'all did a hell of a job capturing that Blad
e runner feel. I wanted a cop type game to get lost in... But a cop game in a dystopian future?? Uh yes please!!!

I know it's in early development but this is money well spent if you're looking for a solid game that seems to get frequent updates and isn't another early access cash grab that never gets completed. You can see the beginning of something truly special. 

Great job developers. Can't wait to see what's in store for this hidden gem.
