reviews.review_description: This open map cyberpunk city is the most fun I have had in a long, long time! I just beat all the teams in Velocity ball and am now grinding to earn the 50,000 credits to get the apt. I’m just so concerned that once I get that done there will be nothing much left to do because Ive been playing everyday for a month haha. I would definitely suggest more interactive places within the lower city and backstreets in future upgrades, but the physics and the gameplay is so very well done and intriguing. Good job you guys! I also love the radio stations. Beat 84 needs some new jams! I’ve memorized em all :)

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! Well done on becoming Velocity Ball champion, I hope you get that apartment 😊 I have plans to make a lot more of the lower city, it all takes time of course, but every day we get closer to our dream! We still love working on expanding and improving the world, so there is still more to come for sure!

reviews.review_description:
This game is actually surprisingly really good. For a low budget looking police simulator, it actually isn't once you play. It's a very well put together game. I bought it, thinking it wouldn't be very good, expecting low quality, but nope. It really turned around. 

Lots of fun missions to do, bad guys to eliminate, credits to collect. Besides the flying cars and super advanced technological city, it's a very realistic law enforcement simulation. I feel like a real cop.

With consistent updates with actual good content, this game is set for great success. Really looking forward to more content to be added.

Very well done to the developer(s) for putting together this great experience of law enforcement. This game has lots of potential.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much! I know the low price often brings low expectations, but I hope it makes more people willing to take a chance on Metacity 🙂

It is really so much for for us adding new content, we can't stop! 😂 Hopefully you'll love what we have coming up next!

reviews.review_description: Love the game wish the combat was done a lil better with there was a radgoll instead of animation, more action shooty missions love you guys

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review and the feedback!

Yeah, the game started out more as a chill experience (and I still want to keep that element), but I'm gradually adding more 'action' elements and aiming to improve on the combat side of things. We always listen to feedback so we'll keep working at it, thanks!

reviews.review_description: Love it, but more than one save slot would be a great addition so I can show it to new users without the vehicle speed being upgraded

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review! I've thought about adding save slots, though mostly the game progression is linear which made me think one slot should be enough (maybe I need to think again!). 

In the meantime, you can slow the car back down using 'Aircar Settings > Disable Speed Upgrades' on the car dashboard. Or if you want to skip back to play from the beginning, you can use the 'Reset' button at HQ (this won't affect your current save, unless you save the game after hitting reset!).

reviews.review_title: First game to surprise me in a while
reviews.review_description:
I will echo most other reviews: this game is surprisingly fun and immersive considering that it isn’t a AAA title which is most of what I play. The flying is fun and relaxing and the game is very atmospheric and easy to get lost in. The strobe effect in the night club is awesome and a clever way to make it feel more real. I would like a few improvements in the following areas but honestly for the price and dev team behind it I’m impressed. For the price, especially with a discount code, this seems like a no brainedt keep in your library.      

Additions I’d like to see: 

- more dialogue or even just lore in writing - it would be cool to learn more about the world, locations and NPCs. 
- the dashboard feels a little too high and I wish there were options to adjust height in the car in addition to moving backward and forward.
- make the synthetics more obviously different from other NPCs. It took me a minute to figure out running NPC = synthetic. 
- some kind of scanner or detective view would be cool -it would help make the synthetics more obvious and be a chance to add some more lore. The role playing potential in this game is huge but I would like it to be more informed by even just environmental story telling. There is more of that in some missions than others but it’s a bit bare bones
